assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only analysis/report files: report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md. No source code files (.rs, .toml, etc.) were created or modified. The report content is purely analytical with no indication of code modification actions taken.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and a summary statement but contains no merge action, no 'gh pr merge' invocation, and no auto-merge trigger. The report ends with '*This report was AI-generated by sdlc-workflow/verify-pr*', presenting findings for human review only.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification is produced by the Style/Conventions sub-agent (which spawns a test classification sub-agent for modified files) — the classification verdict is attributed to the Style/Conventions domain in the report
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 90: The Test Change Classification section appears under '### Style/Conventions'. Line 124 explicitly states: 'Attribution: This classification is produced by the Style/Conventions domain analysis.'

assertion_results.text: The test change classification analysis is based on comparing base-branch and PR-branch file content (function additions, removals, assertion changes) — the structural and semantic assessment references test file content, not acceptance criteria or task requirements (constraint 1.18)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 137-138: 'Comparison of base-branch version (from test-base-purl-recommend.md) vs PR version (from pr-diff-test-changes.md)' -- explicitly comparing base-branch and PR-branch file content. Lines 155-156: 'from a pure test-coverage perspective (independent of task requirements, per constraint 1.18)' -- directly referencing constraint 1.18. The structural summary references specific test file content (function names, assertion strings, PURL literals) rather than acceptance criteria.
